notes.text: The hermetic LLVM variant fixes glib Python codegen and uses preinstalled `make`/`pkg-config`, but libxml2 still builds through `rules_foreign_cc` outside Bazel's C++ rule flow; the zero-sysroot hermetic clang cannot find crt objects such as `Scrt1.o`. Fails identically in both environments.
No hermetic LLVM variant is tracked yet; this large project is kept out of the regular hermetic sweeps.
Its BCR `sed` dependency uses gnulib code that does not parse under clang 22 (`_GL_ATTRIBUTE_FORMAT_PRINTF_STANDARD`). Fails identically in both environments.
The nested `examples/` module still selects `/bin/gcc` on RBE instead of the injected hermetic LLVM toolchain, so the example test fails to compile. Passes locally, where `/bin/gcc` exists.

The nested Go module pulls in the parent C brotli library, but that nested module still selects host C/C++ tools on RBE (`/bin/gcc`/`cc`), so the hermetic LLVM variant fails before the Go tests build. Passes locally, where host tools exist.
The as-is local wrapper reaches Copybara analysis, but `rules_java` selects `local_jdk` inside the sandbox and analysis fails before upstream test targets are found.

The hermetic LLVM variant (with remote JDK flags) runs the full suite locally; only `//javatests/com/google/copybara/git:GitRepositoryTest_all_tests` fails in the sandbox. The same suite passes on RBE.
The Go tests pass (8 / 8), but top-level Go binaries built during the sweep fail to link through hermetic clang with non-PIC relocation errors from `ld.lld` in both environments.

The as-is local suite has 50 runnable Bazel test targets: 49 pass and 1 fails to build; another 50 Windows-only targets are skipped and excluded from the denominator. GCC 13.3 rejects `//test/core/promise:inter_activity_mutex_fuzzer` because its `Poll()` member changes the meaning of the unqualified `Poll<Lock>` type (`-Wchanges-meaning`).
The as-is local Bazel test target fails. The underlying gtest binary runs 91 cases and reports 85 passing and 6 failing, all in stack-trace resolution/current-trace behavior.
The hermetic LLVM variant builds, but the upstream `//test:unittest` fails, matching the as-is stack-trace resolution failures.

The transitive `toolchains_llvm` clang used by dependencies such as `xz` and `zstd` needs `libtinfo.so.5` on the RBE image; `rules_foreign_cc` make bootstrap also fails because that compiler cannot create executables.
The Go tests pass, but `@fzf//:src_test` fails to build: cgo-style Go linking through hermetic `ld.lld` hits non-PIC relocation errors (`R_X86_64_64 ... recompile with -fPIC`) in both environments.

One unit test target passes, but several LLVM unit test targets fail to build: `SupportHelpers.h` includes private `gtest/gtest-printers.h`, and later Hexagon code hits libc++ comparator errors involving deleted `operator<` for `llvm::rdf::RegisterRef`. Fails identically in both environments.

The hermetic build issues are fixed, but the upstream smoke test drives `rsyslogd` with `nc -u -w 0`, which only the netcat-openbsd CLI accepts; the executor images provide a different variant. Passes locally, where netcat-openbsd is installed.
All three regression tests fail to build because Verilator binaries link with `-latomic` and hermetic `ld.lld` finds no `libatomic` on either the RBE image or the local host.
